text:
Eloise was tired, but she had to wake up the kids to run an errand. The kids were asleep in their beds. Well, not everyone. One was snoring and another was restless.

She quietly crawled out of bed and made her way to Olivia's room. The snorer was obviously enjoying her peaceful slumber. Eloise rolled her eyes at the quiet boy who’d kicked off her covers just long enough to steal a peek at an adorable book about unicorns.

“Wake up, Whiskers,” she murmured. “Time to get going.”

On her morning walk, Eloise passed Tom’s house. His front lawn was neatly mowed and sprinklers were running. “Good morning, Tom!”

No reply. Usually she’d hear his cheerful voice. But today seemed heavy.

Entering Tom’s house, she found him hunched over his laptop, lost in thoughts. “Hey Tom,” she said softly. “Need someone to cheer you up?”

A long sigh
